title: Secrets of the Jedi (novel)
content:
{{Top|rwm|imp|italics=1}} {{Otheruses|Secrets of the Jedi}} {{Book |image=[[File:SecretsoftheJedi.png]] |title=''Secrets of the Jedi'' |author=[[Jude Watson]] |cover artist=*[[David Mattingly]] {{C|Illustration art}} *[[Keirsten Geise]] {{C|Design}} |illustrator= |editor= |publisher=[[Scholastic]] |early release= |release date=[[March]] [[2005]]{{SWArchive|url=eu/lit/young/news20040930.html|text=''Secrets of the Jedi'' Revealed}} |intl release= |rereleased= |media type=Hardcover |pages=208 |isbn=9780439536677 |isbn2= |isbn3= |timeline=*[[39 BBY/Legends|39 BBY]] {{C|Part One}}''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' *c. [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] {{C|Part Two}} |series= |preceded by=''[[Legacy of the Jedi]]'' |followed by= }} {{Quote|Qui-Gon and Tahl.
Obi-Wan and Siri.
Anakin and Padmé.
Three generations of Jedi.
Three untold secrets.|Back cover|[[Secrets of the Jedi (novel)|''Secrets of the Jedi'']]}} '''''Secrets of the Jedi''''' is a [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] junior novel written by [[Jude Watson]]. Like its predecessor, ''[[Legacy of the Jedi]]'', it explores the relationships between three generations of [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], and it prominently features [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]], [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], and [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]]. ''Secrets of the Jedi'' was published in [[March]] [[2005]] by [[Scholastic]], and it was [[Legacy of the Jedi/Secrets of the Jedi omnibus|republished]] with ''Legacy of the Jedi'' in [[2006]]. ==Publisher's summary== ''THREE GENERATIONS OF JEDI''. ''THREE UNTOLD SECRETS.'' ''A Jedi is never to fall in love....'' ''Grieving for the loss of his friend Tahl, Jedi Master Qui-Gon Jinn has been sent back to the Jedi Temple to recover. But this peace is soon shattered by an urgent mission — a young boy with a knack for technology has intercepted a message that indicates a mass assassination of planetary leaders is about to take place. It is up to Qui-Gon and his apprentice, Obi-Wan Kenobi, to protect the boy from the bounty hunter assassins…and to foil the deadly plot.'' ''Joining Qui-Gon and Obi-Wan on their mission are the Jedi Master Adi Gallia and her headstrong apprentice, Siri. Obi-Wan and Siri have been sparring friends for a long time. But as the assassins' pursuit tightens, they find their relationship beginning to change in an unexpected direction....'' ''A generation later, Obi-Wan, Siri, and the boy they tried to save are reunited once more — with the fate of the Clone Wars hinging on the outcome. Old tensions rise once more — as well as new conflicts and secrets. Obi-Wan's apprentice, Anakin Skywalker, has many things to hide, and when his secret wife, Padmé Amidala, joins the Jedi mission, the risks become even greater than before.'' ''SECRETS ARE DANGEROUS.'' ''SECRETS ARE DIFFICULT.'' ''SECRETS CAN KILL.'' ==Plot summary== THE PAST: [[Talesan Fry]], a young technology whiz, has intercepted a message revealing that twenty [[Planet/Legends|planetary]] leaders are being targeted for [[Death/Legends|assassination]] by [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] hired by the [[Corporate Alliance/Legends|Corporate Alliance]]. The [[Jedi Council/Legends|Jedi Council]] sends the team of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Masters]] [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]] and [[Adi Gallia/Legends|Adi Gallia]], and their respective [[Padawan/Legends|Padawans]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and [[Siri Tachi/Legends|Siri Tachi]], to protect young Fry. Although the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] are able to thwart the assault, they are unable to stop the vengeful bounty hunter [[Magus (bounty hunter)|Magus]] from killing Fry’s [[Parent/Legends|parents]] in retribution. During the course of the assignment, Obi-Wan and Siri discover their deep love for each other, but they refuse to break the [[Jedi Code/Legends|Jedi Code]], and are forced to turn their backs on their feelings. THE PRESENT: [[Standard year/Legends|Years]] later, during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], Talesan Fry emerges as a leading technological innovator and the mastermind behind the [[Fry Industries]]. He develops a powerful [[Code breaker chip|code breaker]] that he offers to sell to the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] in exchange for a lucrative [[Republic Military/Legends|military]] contract. Obi-Wan Kenobi, his Padawan [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]], Siri Tachi and [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]] venture to [[Genian]] to broker the deal. After ferreting a [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist]] [[Spy/Legends|spy]] from within the company, the Jedi bring Fry to a secret Republic outpost on [[Azure/Legends|Azure]] to activate his code-breaking device. The device has been secretly rigged with a tracker that alerts the [[Confederate Navy/Legends|Separatist fleet]] to their whereabouts. Among the Separatists is Magus, who once again guns for Fry. In a wildly dangerous attack, Siri Tachi leaps from her [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] onto Magus’ [[Starship/Legends|vessel]] during an aerial pursuit and downs the hunter’s ship. The Jedi capture Magus, but Siri tragically dies from [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] wounds suffered at his hands. The Republic has no choice but to destroy the code breaker, least it falls into Separatist hands. ==Development== The working title for ''Secrets of the Jedi'' was ''Jedi Quest Special Edition''. ==Continuity== The novel has two parts, with the second part being set "twenty [[Standard year/Legends|years]]" after the first. The [[2005]] book ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' originally placed the story's latter half in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]],''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' and the [[2012]] book ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' ultimately dated it to around [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]]. When the [[Prequel trilogy]] was in production, the editors at [[Lucasfilm]] restricted authors from including [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]] in the spin-off fiction, particularly in the period between [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|Episodes II]] and [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|III]], as her [[Reproduction/Legends|pregnancy]] was not yet known to many beyond [[George Lucas]]. As such, her appearance in ''Secrets of the Jedi'' was a rare exception. Padmé was more routinely featured in [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] material published after the launch of the [[Star Wars: Clone Wars|animated series]] in [[2008]]. Among the bounty hunters in [[Magus (bounty hunter)|Magus]]' posse was [[Gorm (cyborg)|Gorm the Dissolver]], a [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborg]] first created as a background character in [[Dark Horse Comics|Dark Horse Comics’]] [[Star Wars: Dark Empire|''Dark Empire'']] series ([[1991]]-[[1992]]). The story was originally dated as occurring at twenty-nine [[Standard month/Legends|months]] – over two years – after the start of the Clone Wars, but since [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin]] was presented as a [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]], it must have occurred within the first four [[Standard week/Legends|weeks]] of the war. Early published timelines listed the book as ''Jedi Quest Special Edition'', which was a placeholder title. The novel featured the appearance of a non-[[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[General/Legends|general]] named [[Solomahal/Legends|Solomahal]], who was an original [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''A New Hope'']] ([[1977]]) [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]] [[Alien/Legends|alien]] once known only as “The Colonel”. The [[1996]] customizable card game from [[Decipher|Decipher, Inc.]], gave the character his name and revealed that he was a Clone Wars veteran. ''Secrets of the Jedi'' shrank the scope of character connections by revealing that [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] had previously worked with Solomahal, meaning he was in the cantina with a fellow veteran during the events of the original ''Star Wars''. ==Media== {{Mediacat|from=1|imagecat=Images from Secrets of the Jedi (novel)}} ===Editions=== *{{ISBN|9780439536677}}; [[March]]
